revision.text:
[[ja:芭蕉塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Basho-zuka Kofun |image=[[File:Basho-zuka Kofun, zenkei-3.jpg|280px]]
Mound (Foreground right: posterior circular portion, background left: anterior portion) | part_of= {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} |location={{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|qid=Q817276|lt=Joyo|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, Hirakawa Chayauri |coordinates={{Coord|34.867569|N|135.777953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: dd4 m |built= Mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |burial=[[Clay coffin chamber]] |Buried individual= |burial goods= Armor, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Historic Site= National Historic Site "Basho-zuka Kofun" (part of the "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster") |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label= Basho-zuka Kofun |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-SSW |map_dot_label= top |architectural_styles= {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Basho-zuka Kofun''' (芭蕉塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in {{ill|Jōyō, Kyoto|en|Jōyō, Kyoto|qid=Q817276|lt=Joyo|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site. == Overview == Basho-zuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped kofun constructed on the hilltop along the right bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|qid=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}} in northern Joyo City. The origin of the name "Basho-zuka" is unknown{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. Over 100 Kofuns, including three large kofun—the Basho-zuka Kofun, {{ill|Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|ja|丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|qq=}}—are distributed in this area as part of the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|p=7|qq=}}. The mound is now covered by bamboo groves, and multiple excavation surveys have been conducted{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. The mound has a keyhole type with the anterior portion oriented southward. It is a two-tiered structure{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. The mound measures approximately dd4 meters in length, making it the second largest kofun in the cluster, following the Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun. Protrusions are present on both sides of the constricted portion of the mound{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. Features such as [[fukiishi]] and haniwa (cylindrical, house-shaped, lid-shaped, quiver-shaped, shield-shaped, armor-shaped, and enclosure-shaped) have been identified on the mound{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. Notably, enclosure-shaped haniwa were placed in the valleys between the posterior portion and protrusions, suggesting that these valleys were designed as ritual spaces symbolizing water springs{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. The mound is encircled by a shield-shaped moat, remnants of which remain on the southern side. Rows of large cylindrical haniwa were also found atop the outer embankment{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. The burial facility is a clay coffin burial located in the center of the posterior portion and aligned parallel to the mound's main axis{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=37-39|qq=}}. It is noted that the burial facilities within the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster rarely used stone, a characteristic that continues even in this significant leader's tomb{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. The clay coffin was subjected to large-map_size looting, and fragments of armor believed to be part of the burial goods were recovered from the looting pits{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=37-39|qq=}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the mid-[[Kofun period]]. The external features of this kofun exhibit typical characteristics of mid-Kofun period Kofuns{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}. It is considered the final tomb constructed for a major leader in the Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster, succeeding the Marutsuka and Kutsukawa Kurazuka Kofun{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|p=7|qq=}}. In {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}, the kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the National Historic Site "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster.". == History == * {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}} (Showa 37): Survey of the mound conducted by the {{ill|Archaeological Society of Doshisha University|ja|同志社大学考古学研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archaeological Society of Doshisha University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. * {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Showa 51): Boundary confirmation survey conducted by the Joyo City Board of Education{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. * {{ill|1983|en|1983|qid=Q2434|lt=1983|1=1983}}–{{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} (Showa 58–Heisei 6): Intermittent trial excavations conducted due to development (Joyo City Board of Education){{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. * {{ill|1987|en|1987|qid=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Showa 62): Designated as a Joyo City Historic Site on April 21.Historic Site Explanation Board. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|qid=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}}–{{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 12–17): Excavations conducted in preparation for historical site development (Joyo City Board of Education){{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=9-12|qq=}}. * {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}} (Heisei 28): Designated as part of the National Historic Site "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" on October 3.{{National Cultural Property Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}{{PDFlink|[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/pdf/2016061701_besshi03.pdf Regarding Designations of Historic Sites]|qq=}} (Agency for Cultural Affairs, June 17, 2016).Heisei 28 Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 144, October 3. * {{ill|2024|en|2024|qid=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}} (Reiwa 6): Additional Historic Site of the historical site boundary on October dd. == Mound == The area of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|pp=66-74|qq=}}: * Total length including moat: 161 meters * Mound length: dd4 meters * Posterior portion: Two-tiered structure ** Diameter: 62.7 meters * Anterior portion: Two-tiered structure ** Length: 51.5 meters ** Width: 61.0 meters Basho-zuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|Top of the posterior portion Basho-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View of the anterior portion from the posterior portion == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Basho-zuka Kofun (part of the {{ill|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|ja|久津川古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}}) *: Designated on October 3, 2016, as part of the National Historic Site "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster," which was expanded to include the Basho-zuka and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11426315}}.. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Literature == * Historic Site Explanation Board (Joyo City Board of Education) * Publications by local governments ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|title=Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report (Joyo City Archaeological Report Vol. 51)|publisher=Joyo City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Basho-zuka Kofun Excavation Report|2006|qq=}}|qq=}} ** {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2007|title={{PDFlink|[http://www.kyotofu-maibun.or.jp/data/kankou/kankou-pdf/jyouhou/J103.pdf Kyoto Prefecture Archaeological Report No. 103]|qq=}}|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Archaeological Center|pages=39-40|ref={{Harvid|Kyoto Archaeological Report No. 103|2007|qq=}}|qq=}}. * Encyclopedias ** {{Cite book|author=|year=1981|title=Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|series=Japanese Historical Placenames Series Vol. 26|publisher=Heibonsha|ref={{Harvid|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster (Heibonsha)|1981|qq=}}|qq=}} ** {{Cite book|author=Koizumi Yuji|year=2002|title=Basho-zuka Kofun|series=Continued Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|ref={{Harvid|Basho-zuka Kofun (Continued Encyclopedia)|2002|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Basho-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|1741|Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō City)|Maruzuka Kofun (Jōyō)]] ...{{ill|Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|久津川車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzugawa Kurumazuka Kofun|1=Kutsukawa Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369464}}, and {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|'''芭蕉塚古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, are distributed throughout the area. ...to "Kutsukawa Kofun Cluster" after the addition of {{ill|Basho-zuka Kofun|ja|'''芭蕉塚古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bashōzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja|久世小学校古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuse Elementary School Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 城陽市立久世小学校|1=Kuse Elementary School Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11426315}}Investigations began in 1894 (Meiji 27 ... 5 KB (652 words) - 04:51, 16 January 2025 * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} ...fun]]|[[:ja:丸塚古墳 (城陽市)|丸塚古墳]]|qq=}}, {{nihongo|[[Basho-zuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:'''芭蕉塚古墳'''|'''芭蕉塚古墳''']]|qq=}}, and {{nihongo|[[Kuse Shōgakkō Kofun]]|[[:ja:城陽市立久世小学校#久世小学校古墳|久世小学校古墳] ... 63 KB (7,178 words) - 02:36, 16 January 2025 {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|芭蕉塚古墳|version=104621341|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Basho-zuka Kofun}} [[ja:芭蕉塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28608903}} [[Category:久津川古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]]
